events.source_quote: At a January 28, 2014 board meeting, our board of directors discussed our year to date performance, our anticipated performance against the fiscal year 2014 plan and various factors that could impact our future performance. Following discussions, our board of directors decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.

source_quote: Following such discussions, our board of directors unanimously approved an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch providing for
BofA Merrill Lynch to act as our financial advisor in connection with reviewing our strategic alternatives.

events.bid_date_rough: during the next several weeks (from 2014-02-27 board authorization)
events.source_quote: In all, twenty-five potential buyers informed us that they were not interested in pursuing a potential transaction and declined to enter into a confidentiality agreement with us, and twenty-five potential buyers executed confidentiality agreements with us, received a copy of the marketing materials and/or met with the Company's management.

events.source_quote: One of the financial buyers contacted during such time was New Mountain Capital. We entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital on March 19, 2014.

On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest. The bids received in the preliminary indications of interest ranged from $20.00 per share to $22.00 per share.

flags.reason: aggregate range $20.00-$22.00 across 5 April-14 IOIs; per-bidder breakdown not narrated
one of 5 April-14 IOI submitters (4F + 1S); individual per-share not narrated

events.bid_date_rough: at the IOI deadline (2014-04-14)
events.source_quote: While New Mountain Capital received the marketing materials and the first round process letter, it decided at the time not to submit a preliminary indication of interest.

flags.reason: filing says NMC 'decided at the time not to submit' without explicit date; anchored to 2014-04-14 IOI deadline

events.drop_reason_note: Voluntary: NMC received first-round process letter but decided not to submit a preliminary indication of interest at the April 14, 2014 deadline.

events.source_quote: On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six. The bid submitted by Party X ranged from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.

flags.reason: range $21.50-$23.00 stated in filing

Party X contacted BofA unsolicited and submitted IOI without signing an NDA; later withdrew 2014-05-14 prior to data-room access: 'Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested'

events.source_quote: May 14, 2014, prior to receiving access to the electronic data room or meeting with the Company's management, Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

flags.reason: Party X never signed an NDA and voluntarily withdrew: 'Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested'

events.drop_reason_note: Voluntary: Party X informed BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction.

events.source_quote: On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven. The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share. Access to the electronic data room was subsequently made available to Party Y and the Company's management met with Party Y and provided a presentation about our business.

flags.reason: range $19.50-$20.50 stated in filing
Party Y submitted IOI on 2014-05-20 before signing an NDA; filing narrates data-room access granted 'subsequently' to the May 20 IOI, implying later NDA execution

events.bid_date_rough: subsequently (after 2014-05-20 Party Y IOI, prior to data-room access)
events.source_quote: On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven. The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share. Access to the electronic data room was subsequently made available to Party Y and the Company's management met with Party Y and provided a presentation about our business.

flags.reason: filing does not state Party Y NDA date; inferred from narrative 'Access to the electronic data room was subsequently made available to Party Y' — data-room access implies NDA signed after 2014-05-20 IOI

events.bid_date_rough: over the next few weeks (from 2014-05-22 draft merger agreement distribution)
events.source_quote: Over the next few weeks, five of the remaining six interested parties communicated to representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that they were unable to proceed with the process due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities. The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.

flags.reason: phrase: 'Over the next few weeks'
one of 5 parties (of 6 remaining) that communicated inability to proceed due to valuation concerns

events.drop_reason_note: Voluntary: unable to proceed due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities.

events.drop_reason_note: Voluntary non-response: sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.

events.source_quote: At a June 26, 2014 meeting of our board of directors, based on the lack of buyer interest and the uncertainty surrounding the impact of the fire at our aerosol manufacturing facility in Marietta, Georgia, our board of directors decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time and to continue to focus on our business and financial performance.

In early February 2015, New Mountain Capital contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and requested a meeting. On February 10, 2015, New Mountain Capital met with representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and expressed its interest in discussions with the Company regarding a potential transaction.

flags.reason: NMC previously dropped in phase 1 (2014-04-14) by declining to submit a preliminary indication of interest; returned in early February 2015 to restart process

events.source_quote: On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25. The indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, contained a request for a forty-five day exclusivity period and contemplated a "go-shop" period following the signing of any definitive merger agreement.

flags.reason: NMC delivered concrete price indication ($19.25) in phase 2 before the Feb 27 NDA revival per §M4; later-phase NDA row satisfies §P-D6 existence

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: 'non-binding indication of interest' form; highly confident letter from Jefferies Finance LLC provides financing-strength signal but no firm commitment letters; request for 45-day exclusivity and go-shop in any definitive agreement.

events.source_quote: On February 26, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest reflecting an increased per share price of $20.05, and indicated that this was the highest price it was willing to offer. As part of its submission of the revised indication of interest, New Mountain Capital again requested a forty-five day exclusivity period (and contemplated a countersignature by the Company), and reiterated that it would be willing to accept a "go-shop" provision in any definitive merger agreement.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: still 'revised indication of interest' form (not binding); 'highest price willing to offer' is a strong-price signal but no merger agreement draft or firm commitment letters yet. Exclusivity granted ~30 days following this bid.

events.source_quote: On February 27, 2015, we signed an agreement with New Mountain Capital extending the term of the confidentiality provision, the "standstill" provision and the employee non-solicitation provision in our original confidentiality agreement with them as well as providing for an exclusivity period with New Mountain Capital through March 31, 2015.

flags.reason: phase-2 revival of original NMC NDA signed 2014-03-19 (phase 1); filing narrates Feb 27, 2015 agreement 'extending the term of the confidentiality provision...in our original confidentiality agreement'

events.additional_note: reiteration of Feb 26 IOI as 'best and final'; requested extension of exclusivity through 2015-04-15
events.source_quote: On March 13, 2015, New Mountain Capital communicated that the $20.05 per share offer price was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer. New Mountain Capital also indicated a willingness to work expeditiously towards the signing of a definitive merger agreement by April 15, 2015 and requested an extension of its exclusivity period through that date.

flags.reason: filing uses 'best and final' (§G1 formal trigger) on a reiteration row; classified informal per process-position fallback because instrument form is still pre-merger-agreement IOI

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 process-position: pre-merger-agreement draft; reiterates $20.05 as 'best and final'. Classified informal because no merger-agreement markup or firm commitment letters yet; 'best and final' formal trigger here reiterates the Feb 26 IOI rather than submitting a new binding instrument.

events.additional_note: revised draft merger agreement reduced go-shop from 60 to 30 days; price unchanged at $20.05
events.source_quote: On March 29, 2015, we received a revised draft of the merger agreement from New Mountain Capital. Among other things, the revised merger agreement reduced the "go-shop" period from sixty days to thirty days.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: 'markup of the merger agreement' trigger — NMC delivered a revised draft merger agreement (reducing go-shop from 60 to 30 days); ongoing negotiation of definitive agreement terms (termination fees, exclusivity, no-shop).

events.process_conditions_note: 30-day go-shop period; target termination fee $17.5M (post go-shop) and $8.75M (during go-shop); reverse termination fee $33.75M payable by New Mountain Capital. 58 parties contacted during go-shop; no competing proposals received.
source_quote: Following the meeting of our board of directors, the parties executed the merger agreement and the related transaction documents and issued a press release announcing the transaction on the morning of April 8, 2015.

As of the end of the go-shop period, none of the parties contacted during the go-shop process had submitted a competing acquisition proposal to us or our representatives, and no such party remained engaged in discussions or negotiations with us or our representatives with respect to a potential acquisition proposal.

Upon the consummation of the merger, each share of our common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger (other than shares we own or shares owned by Parent or by holders who are entitled to, and who properly exercise, appraisal rights under Delaware law) will be converted into the right to receive $20.05 in cash.
